text.title: adobe-firefly-integration Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for adobe-firefly-integration as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Agent assumes a public Firefly API exists and attempts direct generation calls without enterprise access
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: There is no public Firefly API for individuals. Attempts to call it will fail or use unofficial workarounds. Agents must check for enterprise access before attempting API automation.

failure-mode.description: Agent runs a batch job requesting many variants per asset without checking available credit balance
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Credits are consumed per variation. A large batch job can exhaust monthly allowance. Always validate credit balance and estimate consumption before scheduling batch generation.

failure-mode.description: Agent treats identical prompts as deterministic and stores only one variant
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Firefly output is non-deterministic. Store all generated variants with their prompts; human-select before publishing. Never assume the first generated output is production-ready.

failure-mode.description: Agent interprets silent rejection (Firefly content moderation error) as a network error and retries without modifying the prompt
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Firefly silently rejects certain subjects without explanation. Agents must detect rejection-type errors, rephrase the prompt (remove triggering elements), and retry with modified content.

failure-mode.description: Agent validates generated assets against brand color by visual description alone
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Color descriptions in prompts are interpreted loosely. Post-processing color validation requires programmatic comparison (colormath or ImageMagick) against brand palette values, not visual description matching.

failure-mode.description: Firefly-generated assets used in production without human review
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Firefly outputs trend toward Adobe Stock aesthetic and may not match brand personality. Always involve a human art director in the selection and approval process before any asset goes to production.

failure-mode.description: Typography and text effects from Illustrator used directly as production SVG icons without manual refinement
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Firefly's text-to-vector produces decorative vectors with complex, non-editable paths unsuitable for UI iconography. Proofs in Illustrator at intended print or screen size are required; manual path simplification is often necessary.
